skills.name: WCAG 2.2 A/AA Compliance Requirements
skills.description: WCAG 2.2 success criteria for A and AA conformance. Includes new 2.2 criteria: focus appearance, dragging alternatives, target size.
skills.template:
## WCAG 2.2 — Key A/AA Requirements

### Level A (must meet)
- 1.1.1 Non-text content: `alt` on images, captions on video
- 1.3.1 Info and relationships: semantic HTML, not just visual styling
- 1.4.1 Use of color: don't convey info by color alone
- 2.1.1 Keyboard: all functionality operable via keyboard
- 2.4.2 Page titled: meaningful `<title>` on every page
- 3.3.1 Error identification: describe errors in text, not just color

### Level AA (must meet)
- 1.4.3 Contrast: text ≥ 4.5:1, large text ≥ 3:1
- 1.4.4 Resize text: 200% zoom without horizontal scroll
- 1.4.10 Reflow: single-column layout at 320px wide
- 1.4.11 Non-text contrast: UI components ≥ 3:1 against adjacent
- 2.4.7 Focus visible: keyboard focus indicator must be visible
- 3.1.2 Language of parts: `lang` attribute on foreign-language sections

### New in WCAG 2.2
- 2.4.11 **Focus Appearance**: focus indicator ≥ 2px outline, ≥ 3:1 contrast against background
- 2.5.7 **Dragging Movements**: all drag actions have single-pointer alternative
- 2.5.8 **Target Size**: interactive targets ≥ 24×24 CSS px (AA)
- 3.3.7 **Redundant Entry**: don't re-ask for info already provided
- 3.3.8 **Accessible Authentication**: no cognitive test for login (or provide alternative)

### Testing tools
```bash
npm install -D @axe-core/playwright axe-core
# Automated: catches ~30–40% of WCAG issues
# Manual: keyboard nav, screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver) required for remainder
```

skills.name: ARIA Roles, Landmarks, and Labels
skills.description: Use ARIA landmark roles and attributes correctly. Prefer native HTML semantics over ARIA. Rule: no ARIA is better than wrong ARIA.
skills.template:
## ARIA Landmarks and Roles ### Landmark regions (use native HTML first) ```html

Products

``` ### aria-label vs aria-labelledby vs aria-describedby ```html

Settings

Minimum 8 characters

``` ### Live regions for dynamic updates ```html
{statusMessage}
{errorMessage}
``` ### ARIA rules 1. Use native HTML over ARIA always 2. Don't add ARIA to native elements that already have the role 3. All interactive ARIA elements must be keyboard operable 4. Don't use `aria-hidden="true"` on focusable elements

skills.name: Keyboard Navigation — Focus Management
skills.description: Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible. Implement roving tabindex, skip links, and visible focus indicators.

skills.name: Accessible Form Patterns
skills.description: Every form control must have an accessible name. Error messages must be programmatically associated. Required fields must be announced.

skills.name: Color Contrast and Motion Accessibility
skills.description: Meet WCAG contrast ratios and respect prefers-reduced-motion for users with vestibular disorders.

skills.name: Accessible Images and Media
skills.description: Write meaningful alt text, handle decorative images, add captions to video, and make SVGs accessible.

skills.name: Focus Management in Modals and Dialogs
skills.description: Trap focus inside open dialogs, restore focus on close, and use inert to prevent background interaction.
